channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-12834] MDT hung during failover
item.description:
1 MDT got stuck after it came back from a reboot. This issue looks like LU-12354, not sure if it is dup

[2019-10-04T18:13:18+00:00] INFO: template[/etc/ssh/sshd_config] sending restart action to service[sshd] (delayed)
[2019-10-04T18:13:18+00:00] INFO: Processing service[sshd] action restart (ssh::server line 19)
[2019-10-04T18:13:18+00:00] INFO: service[sshd] restarted

[2019-10-04T18:13:18+00:00] INFO: Report handlers complete

[  238.891233] alg: No test for adler32 (adler32-zlib)

 192.168.1.109@o2ib

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: Hmm, it looks like the LU-11285 patch was abandoned. Trying to figure out the details.

According to comments in LU-12035, it was the patch https://review.whamcloud.com/33077 "LU-11285 ldlm: reprocess whole waiting queue for IBITS" that resolved this issue.

I would recommend to re-enable the remote rename on soak, so that we can see if this issue is fixed properly, since disabling remote rename is at best a temporary workaround.
Yes, it would be better to disable rename between MDTs before it's fixed thoroughly.
Ah I see. So you are suggesting that we utilize this option on soak?

the patch https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/34410/ in LU-12037 added an option to disable the rename between MDTs,
but the default value is "1", which allow the rename between MDTs. the customer tried the patch in their site to disable
the rename operation between MDTs, which avoids the issue to occur.
hongchao.zhang you mean that the fix is not 100% effective?
It could be related to LU-12037, which cause deadlock when rename between MDTs.
